title: fix(supervisor): quarantine state survives deactivate (S6)
description: Plan finding S6. deactivate() marks handle quarantined then live.delete(id) — signal lost. Next activate() re-reads from disk bypassing quarantine. Fix: Map<string, Error> tracks quarantined keys; activate() rejects with original failure reason; clearQuarantine() is explicit unblock. See 000-docs/session-state-machine.md.
close_reason: Fixed: supervisor.ts now tracks quarantined keys in Map<string,Error>; activate() rejects for quarantined keys; deactivate() records to quarantined before live.delete; clearQuarantine() unblocks. 5 new tests pass (370→375). PR #90 https://github.com/jeremylongshore/claude-code-slack-channel/pull/90

title: fix(journal): broken-flag + schema parse ordering (S2 + S3)
description: Plan findings S2 and S3. (S2) broken guard in writeEvent runs at enqueue time; calls already on the queue still execute _doWrite after a prior write fails. Add guard at top of _doWrite. (S3) JournalEvent.parse runs AFTER hash computation; schema rejection on caller input does not mark the writer broken but also does not re-establish a clean retry contract. Move schema validation BEFORE hash computation per audit-journal-architecture.md §190-210 fail-loud contract.
close_reason: shipped in v0.5.1 release commit f146952 — S2/S3 (journal broken-flag + schema ordering, PR #89), S4 (session Zod validation), S6 (supervisor quarantine persistence). Stale in-progress flag only; code delta is on main.

title: fix(sendable): assertSendable state-root denylist (S1)
description: Plan finding S1. CLAUDE.md and THREAT-MODEL.md claim the state directory is blocked from outbound file sends; the code only enforces allowlist + basename/parent denylists. An operator setting SLACK_SENDABLE_ROOTS to any ancestor of ~/.claude/channels/slack exposes access.json and audit.log. Fix: add stateRoot parameter; reject when realpath(file) is under realpath(stateRoot); thread STATE_DIR through from server.ts callers.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #86. Tests: 5 new cases in describe('assertSendable — state-root denylist'): blocks access.json via ancestor allowlist, blocks sessions/C/T.json, blocks symlink-into-state, allows sibling dir, preserves legacy 3-arg behavior. bun test 375 pass / 0 fail (baseline ~370, +5).

title: fix(supervisor): quarantine state survives deactivate (S6)
description: Plan finding S6. deactivate() currently marks a handle quarantined and then calls live.delete(id) — signal is lost. Next activate() re-reads from disk as if nothing happened. Fix: track quarantined keys in a separate Map<string, Error> so activate() can reject with the original failure reason; provide clearQuarantine() as the explicit unblock path. See 000-docs/session-state-machine.md for the state contract.
close_reason: shipped in v0.5.1 release commit f146952 — S2/S3 (journal broken-flag + schema ordering, PR #89), S4 (session Zod validation), S6 (supervisor quarantine persistence). Stale in-progress flag only; code delta is on main.

title: 31-B.5: Gate — only allowFrom callers may publish
description: publish_manifest verifies the caller's user_id is in the top-level allowFrom set. Cross-references existing access control. Non-allowFrom caller rejected with clear error.
close_reason: Publish gate shipped in PR #112. assertPublishAllowed(ownerId, access) in lib.ts throws on any caller not in access.allowFrom. Same allowlist governs DMs and publish (one operator-facing surface). Default-safe: empty allowFrom rejects everyone. 5 tests cover: in-list pass, not-in-list reject with reason, empty-list reject, case-sensitive exact-match, channel-level allowFrom explicitly not consulted. 507/507 tests pass, typecheck clean. Ships before publish_manifest tool so the authorization surface is fixed before any publish code is written.

title: 31-A.4: Invariant — manifest data NEVER passed to evaluate() policy
description: Miller 2006: 'advertisements are not grants.' The peer's manifest is advertising — it can claim anything. Policy decisions MUST use only verified signals (user_id, channel, tool). Add a type-level guard if possible.
close_reason: Invariant shipped in PR #111. policy.ts gets a module-level invariant doc block citing 000-docs/bot-manifest-protocol.md §91-109. server.test.ts gets a two-test import-graph lint (one checks policy.ts has zero manifest-import specifiers; one sanity-proves the parser handles import/import()/require()/re-export/case variants and ignores comment mentions). 502/502 tests pass, typecheck clean. Guard lands before any 31-A code exists, so first violation is a merge block.

title: Kill top-5 Stryker survivors on security primitives
description: The Stryker baseline (PR #128) surfaced a survivor cluster on security-critical surfaces documented in 000-docs/MUTATION_REPORT.md: PERMISSION_REPLY_RE anchor mutants at lib.ts:38 (5 survivors), SENDABLE_BASENAME_DENY / SENDABLE_PARENT_DENY_SINGLE / SENDABLE_PARENT_DENY_PAIRS entries at lib.ts:691-717, the unfurl_links / unfurl_media flag propagation, and pruneExpired TTL boundary. Each survivor represents a test that doesn't fail when the primitive is weakened. Fix approach: write targeted per-entry tests (every regex anchor, every denylist entry) and inject a clock to exercise pruneExpired at the TTL boundary. Target: kill all 5, lift lib.ts mutation score from 79.85% over the 80% high-threshold line.
acceptance_criteria: stryker run on lib.ts reports PERMISSION_REPLY_RE / SENDABLE_*_DENY / unfurl / pruneExpired mutants killed; overall lib.ts score >=80%
close_reason: Shipped in PR #133 (commit b3f5501). 38 targeted tests kill the top-5 Stryker survivor clusters from MUTATION_REPORT.md: 5 PERMISSION_REPLY_RE anchor/charset mutants, SENDABLE_BASENAME_DENY per-entry (17 fixtures + 2 positive controls for the .env regex anchors), SENDABLE_PARENT_DENY single + pair per-entry, pruneExpired Access-pending TTL boundary (Date.now override), isDuplicateEvent TTL boundary, unfurl_links/unfurl_media literals. Stryker re-run on intermediate commit 2c3d71c confirmed 42/45 targeted mutants killed (score 84.45%, up from 79.85% baseline — cleanly above the 80 high threshold). 3 residual mutants (2 .env anchor + 1 Access pruneExpired) addressed in follow-up commit d5b81d7 on same branch.

description: Referenced by xa3.10 and Batch 3 wiring. Currently Promise.reject('not yet implemented') stub in supervisor.ts. xa3.10 cannot land without it. title: fix(session): Zod schema validation in loadSession (S4)
description: Plan finding S4. loadSession casts JSON.parse result with 'as Session' — no runtime validation at the trust boundary. A corrupt or tampered session file with wrong types (e.g. ownerId: 42) passes load and reaches the supervisor. Fix: add SessionSchema (Zod, strict), validate in loadSession, update the doc comment that currently claims 'does NOT schema-validate'.

title: infra: Fail-fast at startup when SLACK_SENDABLE_ROOTS entry missing or inaccessible
description:
lib.ts:284-286 silently falls back to path.resolve() when realpathSync throws on a configured SENDABLE_ROOTS entry. An attacker who creates a symlink at the expected path post-startup could make a previously inaccessible root suddenly accessible with a structurally different (non-canonicalized) check.

Fix: on server.ts bootstrap, call realpathSync on every SENDABLE_ROOTS entry. If any throws, log the failure and exit(1) — don't degrade to lexical resolve(). Failing-closed matches the rest of the security posture.

Acceptance:
- server.ts exits non-zero on missing/inaccessible SENDABLE_ROOTS entry with clear stderr message
- Existing tests still pass (hermetic tests that stub the filesystem continue to work)
- Documented in ACCESS.md / SECURITY.md that SENDABLE_ROOTS must exist at boot
close_reason: Bundled into PR #56. validateSendableRoots() added to lib.ts, called from server.ts bootstrap with exit(1) on failure. 11 new PolicyRule schema tests locking MatchSpec refinement, channel regex, discriminated-union variance, 24h ttlMs ceiling, default priority/ttlMs, parsePolicyRules deferred id-uniqueness. ACCESS.md fail-fast section added. 159 pass/0 fail/334 expects.

title: 29-A.1-followup: Test coverage for PolicyRule Zod schema
description:
Landed 29-A.1 without tests. Add server.test.ts coverage for:

- MatchSpec zero-field refinement rejection (all five fields undefined)
- MatchSpec.argEquals empty object rejected (refinement treats as zero fields)
- ttlMs 24h ceiling enforcement (accepts 24h, rejects 24h+1ms)
- ttlMs default = 5 minutes
- priority default = 100
- Channel regex ^[CD][A-Z0-9]+$ boundaries (one-char tolerated today, flag if we tighten)
- Discriminated-union variance: auto_approve rejects reason/ttlMs, deny requires reason, require_approval accepts ttlMs default
- parsePolicyRules does NOT enforce duplicate id (documents deferred behavior for loader in 29-A.5)

Acceptance: 8+ passing assertions, imports from policy.ts, all paths in parsePolicyRule/parsePolicyRules exercised.
close_reason: Bundled into PR #56. validateSendableRoots() added to lib.ts, called from server.ts bootstrap with exit(1) on failure. 11 new PolicyRule schema tests locking MatchSpec refinement, channel regex, discriminated-union variance, 24h ttlMs ceiling, default priority/ttlMs, parsePolicyRules deferred id-uniqueness. Silent-drop posture pinned at every level: extractManifests drops malformed JSON / Zod-invalid / oversized without surfacing errors; evaluate() has no manifest surface to match on; 31-A.4 import-graph CI check ensures policy.ts never imports manifest module.

title: Manifest schema and the read tool
description: Zod schema with magic header (s53.1) + slack/read_peer_manifests MCP tool (s53.2).
close_reason: Sub-epic complete. Both leaves closed: ccsc-s53.1 (ManifestV1 Zod schema, PR #113) and ccsc-s53.2 (read_peer_manifests MCP tool, PR #114). manifest.ts carries both the frozen schema (with magic-header discriminator) and the pure extractManifests function; server.ts registers the tool with outbound-gate reuse for channel opt-in.

title: Prove the chain catches any tampering
description: End-to-end chain verification over 1000-event log (5pi.8), redaction coverage (5pi.9), tamper-detection via chain verify (5pi.10).
close_reason: All leaf beads closed. Epic 30-A 'Prove' and 'Docs' sub-epics complete.

title: CLI flag and atomic append to disk
description: --audit-log-file + SLACK_AUDIT_LOG wiring (5pi.6) and O_APPEND+fsync-on-rotation writer (5pi.7).
close_reason: CLI flag/env resolver (5pi.6) + atomic O_APPEND+fsync (5pi.7) both landed. All children closed.

title: Scrub tokens and huge payloads before writing
description: Token-pattern redaction (5pi.4) and input truncation (5pi.5). Prevents credential leakage before hash + append.
close_reason: Redaction (5pi.4) + truncation (5pi.5) both landed. All children closed.

title: Append-only log with a SHA-256 chain
description: JournalEvent type (5pi.1), SHA-256 hash-chained writer (5pi.2), trusted-anchor timestamp (5pi.3) — the append-only log structure.
close_reason: Chain + writer + ts/seq + anchor-in-body all landed. All children closed.

title: Track multiple human approvers without loops or bypass
description: Pending-approvers map, user_id dedup, UTC clock injection, startup warnings (me6.4-7). Prevents approval loops and policy bypass.
close_reason: Epic 29-B shipped end-to-end in v0.6.0 via PRs #100, #101, #102, #103, #104. All children closed; changelog + README policy engine section + package.json 0.6.0 landed in #104. Release tag v0.6.0 pushed + GitHub release cut.

title: Integration tests across the approval flow
description: Verify auto_approve bypass, require:N quorum, deny-reason surfaces (me6.8-10).
close_reason: Epic 29-B shipped end-to-end in v0.6.0 via PRs #100, #101, #102, #103, #104. All children closed; changelog + README policy engine section + package.json 0.6.0 landed in #104. Release tag v0.6.0 pushed + GitHub release cut.

title: Hook policy decisions into the server reply path
description: Entry-point integration for 29-B: auto_approve/deny/require branches (me6.1-3) and trace-event emission (me6.11). Ties evaluate() output to runtime behavior.
close_reason: Epic 29-B shipped end-to-end in v0.6.0 via PRs #100, #101, #102, #103, #104. All children closed; changelog + README policy engine section + package.json 0.6.0 landed in #104. Release tag v0.6.0 pushed + GitHub release cut.

title: Evaluate rules and catch bad configurations early
description: Runtime + safety layer for 29-A. evaluate() first-applicable XACML combining (29-A.3), shadow-detection linter (29-A.5), monotonicity invariant check (29-A.6), and the 15+ case test matrix (29-A.7).
close_reason: All four children closed (v1b.3/.5/.6/.7 in PR #58). Sub-epic drained; Epic 29-A (v1b) auto-closes when beads detects all sub-epics done.

title: Define what a policy rule looks like
description: Data-model layer for 29-A. Zod PolicyRule schema (29-A.1), PolicyDecision tagged union (29-A.2), and path canonicalization primitives (29-A.4) — the immutable types evaluate() operates on.
close_reason: All children closed (v1b.1 PolicyRule #47, v1b.2 PolicyDecision + v1b.4 path canon PR #57). Sub-epic drained.

title: 31-B.10: Test — replace semantics: prior manifest unpinned before new one pinned
description: Publish manifest A. Publish manifest B. Assert: at no point are both A and B pinned simultaneously. Assert final state: only B pinned.
close_reason: Replace-sweep filter shipped in PR #123. Extracted findOurPriorManifestPins pure function from server.ts into manifest.ts: takes PinItemLike[] + BotIdentity, returns ts[] of our own prior v1 manifest pins. 10 tests cover: fail-closed on empty identity, empty list, match by bot_id, match by user, skip file-kind pins, skip peer manifests, skip our non-manifest pins, multiple priors in order, skip missing ts, either-identity-alone suffices. publish_manifest handler now delegates filter to the extracted function — shrinks handler + makes replace-semantics testable without WebClient mock.

title: 31-B.9: Test — non-allowFrom caller rejected
description: Call publish_manifest as a user_id not in allowFrom. Assert rejection, assert no Slack API calls made, assert clear error message that does not leak allowFrom contents.
close_reason: Covered by tests shipped in PR #112 (ccsc-0qk.5): assertPublishAllowed describe block — 'rejects user not in access.allowFrom with a clear, ID-bearing error', 'rejects every caller when allowFrom is empty (hardened default)', 'is case-sensitive on user_id (Slack IDs are opaque and exact-match)', 'does not consult per-channel ChannelPolicy.allowFrom'. The whole gate is exercised against both the in-list happy path and 4 distinct reject paths. No new work needed.

title: 31-B.8: Test — rate limiter rejects second publish within 1h
description: Publish at t=0 succeeds. Publish at t=30min throws. Publish at t=61min succeeds. Advance fake clock to test.
close_reason: Covered by tests shipped in PR #121 (ccsc-0qk.4): createPublishRateLimiter describe block — 'second publish within the window throws with an ID- and time-bearing error', 'publish at exactly windowMs from the last is allowed (boundary exclusive)', 'default window is 1 hour when opts.windowMs is omitted'. The default-window test literally proves: first publish → 1h-1ms later throws → 1h later passes. No new work needed.

title: 31-B.7: Test — round-trip publish → re-read returns equivalent manifest
description: Full cycle. Publish via 31-B, read via 31-A, assert deep-equal (modulo publishedAt timestamp). Uses a real Slack mock / recorded response fixture.
close_reason: Round-trip publish → read shipped in PR #123. Pure-function chain test (assertPublishSizeAndSerialize → extractManifests) proves the publisher's on-wire bytes parse back to the same manifest object the read path produces. 3 cases: minimal manifest byte-for-field round-trip, fully-populated manifest (every optional field including agentCard) without loss, 1000-char description fixture pinning the Postel-Law safety margin (8 KB publish body fits in 40 KB read cap with ~5x headroom). No WebClient mock needed — serialize/deserialize equivalence is what matters.

title: 31-B.3: Replace semantics — pins.list → remove prior → post → pins.add
description: Find any existing pinned manifest from this bot_id. pins.remove it. Post new manifest message. pins.add. Atomic-ish (no two manifests pinned at once, worst case transient gap).
close_reason: Replace semantics shipped in PR #119 (paired with ccsc-0qk.1). Before posting, publish_manifest calls pins.list(channel) and filters to messages whose bot_id/user matches ours AND whose text contains the magic header, then pins.remove each prior manifest. Best-effort: pins.list and individual pins.remove failures are logged and skipped so a flaky Slack call never blocks the publish — worst case an extra pin survives until the next publish sweeps it up. Journal event carries replaced-count so operators can verify the sweep ran.

title: 31-B.4: In-memory rate limiter — 1 publish per channel per hour
description: Map<channelId, lastPublishAt>. Reject second publish within 1h with clear error. Prevents pinning spam. Not persisted — restart resets.
close_reason: 1-publish/channel/hour rate limiter shipped in PR #121. PUBLISH_RATE_LIMIT_MS = 3_600_000 ms in manifest.ts + createPublishRateLimiter factory with injected time source, 256-entry soft-LRU, and checkAndRecord() that reserves the slot BEFORE the caller publishes (non-rollback — a retry-on-failure limiter would be trivially bypassable). Wired into server.ts publish_manifest handler between size cap and replace sweep; rejection journals gate.outbound.deny. Rejection error carries channel + elapsed minutes + remaining minutes so operators know when they can retry. 10 tests pin the contract: constant, first-publish, second-within-window, boundary exclusive at windowMs, post-window refresh, channel isolation, clear, soft-LRU eviction, default window, remaining-time format. 574/574 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: 31-B.2: Publish-size cap — 8KB (stricter than read)
description: Enforce 8KB on the serialized manifest at publish time. Stricter than 31-A's 40KB read cap (Postel's Law — strict on output, tolerant on input).
close_reason: 8 KB publish-side size cap shipped in PR #120. MAX_PUBLISH_MANIFEST_BYTES = 8 * 1024 in manifest.ts, stricter than the 40 KB read cap (Postel's Law). assertPublishSizeAndSerialize helper serializes with JSON.stringify(.., null, 2) + measures UTF-8 bytes via TextEncoder — returns the serialized string so the caller can post directly (bytes measured = bytes on wire). server.ts publish_manifest handler calls it after auth gates, reuses the returned string for chat.postMessage, and journals gate.outbound.deny on rejection. 7 tests cover constant value, Postel invariant, normal case, near-cap binary search (cap inclusive), one-byte-over rejection, error-message naming (actual bytes + cap + shrinkable fields), and multi-byte UTF-8 safety. 564/564 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: 31-B.1: slack/publish_manifest(channel, manifest) MCP tool
description: Tool accepts a manifest object, validates it via the same Zod schema as 31-A, and publishes it as a pinned message in the given channel. Only one manifest per bot per channel.
close_reason: publish_manifest MCP tool shipped in PR #119 (paired with ccsc-0qk.3). Tool registered in toolSchemas + ListTools + switch handler. Input schema: channel (public C...), caller_user_id (U...), manifest (full ManifestV1 nested Zod). Flow: assertPublishAllowed gate → assertOutboundAllowed gate → replace sweep → chat.postMessage → pins.add → journal 'manifest.publish'. 7 schema tests pin the input contract. 557/557 tests pass, typecheck clean. Size cap and rate limit layer on in ccsc-0qk.2 and ccsc-0qk.4.

title: 31-A.9: Test — policy engine (29-A) refuses manifest data as input
description: Negative test. Attempt to call evaluate(call, policies, now) where call.manifestClaims is populated. Compile-time error if possible via type. Runtime assert if not.
close_reason: evaluate() no-manifest-surface proof shipped in PR #117. Three tests: (1) compile-time @ts-expect-error on ToolCall literal with manifestClaims field — if ToolCall ever widens, tsc --noEmit goes red; (2) runtime — manifest-shaped fields smuggled into ToolCall.input are ignored by evaluate() since MatchSpec has no key for name/vendor/version; (3) runtime complement — a deny rule is not bypassable by stuffing a manifest payload. 550/550 tests pass, typecheck clean. Pairs with 31-A.4 (PR #111) import-graph structural guard — same invariant proven at three levels (CI import-graph, tsc compile-time, runtime demonstration).

title: 31-A.8: Test — oversized manifest dropped
description: Pin a message that passes schema but body is 50KB. Tool must drop it at the 40KB size cap, before schema validation. Assert [].
close_reason: Already covered by tests shipped in PR #115 (ccsc-s53.3): 'accepts a body at exactly the 40 KB cap (40960 bytes)', 'silently drops a body one byte over the cap (40961 bytes)', and 'size cap counts UTF-8 bytes, not UTF-16 code units (multi-byte safe)'. The oversized case is both silently dropped (returns []) AND emits an operator-actionable console.debug line for DoS signals. No new work needed.

title: 31-A.6: Test — malformed JSON dropped silently, does not crash tool
description: Pin a message containing the magic header but broken JSON. read_peer_manifests must return [] (or skip that entry), not throw. Verify via error capture.
close_reason: Already covered by tests shipped in PR #114 (ccsc-s53.2): extractManifests 'silently drops entries whose JSON fails to parse' and 'preserves input order across a mix of valid and invalid entries' (which uses '{malformed json' as one entry) both pin the malformed-JSON silent-drop posture. The try/catch around JSON.parse in extractManifests means the tool cannot crash on malformed input. No new work needed.

title: 31-A.7: Test — schema-invalid entry dropped, other valid entries returned
description: Pin 3 messages: valid A, schema-invalid B (wrong type in capabilities[]), valid C. Tool returns [A, C], drops B silently, logs debug.
close_reason: Already covered by tests shipped in PR #114 (ccsc-s53.2): extractManifests 'silently drops entries that fail Zod validation' (uses a non-SemVer version to trigger Zod failure) and 'preserves input order across a mix of valid and invalid entries' (valid+malformed+valid returns the two valid ones in order). Both pin the schema-invalid silent-drop + surviving-valid-entries posture. No new work needed.

title: 31-A.2: slack/read_peer_manifests(channel) MCP tool
description: Flow: pins.list(channel) → filter to messages containing the magic header → parse JSON → validate via Zod → collect. Returns [] if no pins, partial on some-valid/some-invalid.
close_reason: read_peer_manifests MCP tool shipped in PR #114. Pure extractManifests() in manifest.ts handles filter/parse/Zod-validate with silent drops per doc §81. server.ts registers the tool (toolSchemas + ListTools entry + switch case), reuses assertOutboundAllowed as read-access gate, fetches pins + last 50 messages per doc §79, returns stable JSON {channel, count, manifests[]} shape. Either fetch failing falls through cleanly. 10 new tests cover empty/no-magic/single/mixed-order/malformed-JSON/Zod-fail/truthy-imposter/null-safe/pre-filter/no-dedup. 533/533 tests pass, typecheck clean. Size cap (s53.3), rate limit+cache (s53.5), and formal malformed-drop tests (s53.13) are explicit out-of-scope for this bead and will layer on without changing the surface.

title: 31-A.3: Size cap — 40KB per manifest on read (silent drop)
description: If a pinned message body exceeds 40KB at the JSON parse step, drop it without error propagation. Log a debug line. Prevents memory-bomb DoS via oversized pin.
close_reason: 40 KB size cap shipped in PR #115. MAX_MANIFEST_BYTES = 40 * 1024 constant exported from manifest.ts. Check uses TextEncoder (UTF-8 bytes, not string.length) so multi-byte code points cannot slip past. Layered in extractManifests after magic-header pre-filter, before JSON.parse — the parser never sees oversized input. Oversize drops emit console.debug (operator-actionable DoS signal); malformed-JSON/Zod-fail drops stay silent per §81. 3 new tests: at-cap 40960 passes, 40961 drops with debug line referencing 'oversized' and the actual byte count, 🔥-padded multi-byte test proves cap is in bytes not characters. 536/536 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: 31-A.1: Zod schema with magic header __claude_bot_manifest_v1__
description: Fields: __claude_bot_manifest_v1__:true, agent:{id,name,version}, capabilities:string[], contact:string, publishedAt:ISO-8601. Header acts as discriminator for pins.list filter.
close_reason: ManifestV1 Zod schema shipped in PR #113. New module manifest.ts encodes the frozen contract from 000-docs/bot-manifest-protocol.md §17-55 exactly: magic header literal true, name/vendor 1..80, SemVer version (build metadata deliberately rejected), description ≤1000, tools[] ≤50 with per-tool name ≤80 and description ≤400, optional channels[] ≤50 public-ID-only (C...), optional email contact, ISO-8601 publishedAt. Also exports MANIFEST_V1_MAGIC_KEY for the read tool (s53.2) and pins.list discriminator. 16 new tests cover every field's bounds and the magic-header truthy-imposter attack. 523/523 tests pass, typecheck clean. The 31-A.4 invariant still holds: policy.ts does not import from manifest.ts.

title: 30-B.10: Test — correlation ID links receipt to permission-relay prompt (requires 29-B)
description: Full flow: policy require:1 → Block Kit posts → approve → tool executes → receipt edited. Correlation ID in both Block Kit context and receipt context. Assert match.
close_reason: Original scope assumed a full round-trip including the post-exec edit that dhh.3 would provide. With dhh.3 deferred to v0.7.x (ccsc-4nm), the correlation-ID linkage a full-flow test would verify (Block Kit prompt cid → receipt cid → edit cid) reduces to: policy.allow journal event carries correlationId (covered by PR #106 wiring). When ccsc-4nm lands it ships an integration test alongside. Reopen or re-file at that point.

title: 30-B.9: Test — audit:off produces zero Slack messages
description: Default state. Submit tool call. Assert chat.postMessage is never called for a receipt. Local log (30-A) should still be written if --audit-log-file is set.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #107 (merged 2026-04-20). Extracted buildAndPostAuditReceipt into lib.ts with dependency-injected postMessage, letting the off/compact/full/error decision matrix be tested without a live WebClient. 8 new tests cover the full matrix including Slack error-string propagation and the 'projection must not block exec' invariant. 491 tests total. 11 Gemini review iterations addressed (most taken, all resolved clean).

title: 30-B.8: Regression test — self-echo on allowBotIds channels stays filtered (locks in #33)
description: Two-bot channel with allowBotIds. Bot A's projection messages must not trigger Bot A's gate() inbound. Locks in the fix from PR #33.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #108 (merged 2026-04-20). +3 regression tests locking in the audit-receipt self-echo drop across all three signals (bot_id+user / bot_profile.app_id / misconfigured allowBotIds). Tests couple to real buildAuditReceiptMessage() output so any future format change re-exercises the self-echo path. 494 tests total.

title: 30-B.6: Graceful failure — Slack API error on journal post is log-only
description: If chat.postMessage / chat.update fails during projection, log and continue. NEVER block tool execution. This is projection — the authoritative log is 30-A's local file.
close_reason: Covered by implementation in PR #106 (dhh.2) and verified by tests in PR #107 (dhh.9). buildAndPostAuditReceipt catches Slack errors via try/catch and reports to onError without throwing; the 'Slack throws — onError fires, result undefined, no throw (projection must not block exec)' test locks in the invariant. The 'projection never blocks tool execution' contract is also documented in audit-journal-architecture.md. When the post-execution edit hook lands (dhh.3 rescoped), it inherits the same log-only discipline via the identical buildAndPostAuditReceipt pattern.

title: 30-B.7: Rate-limit batching — >5 events/sec collapses to summary
description: Token bucket per channel. If burst exceeds 5/s, collapse to a single summary message linking to the local log path. Prevents Slack rate-limit outages from audit traffic.
close_reason: Subsumed by the ccsc-4nm deferral. Rate-limit batching existed to collapse high-volume chat.update traffic from the post-exec edit hook; without the edit hook, the only Slack traffic from projection is one chat.postMessage per approved tool call — same cadence as the journal write, no batching advantage. Revisit when ccsc-4nm ships.

title: 30-B.5: full mode — redacted inputs + outcome (uses 30-A redaction)
description: Pulls redaction module from 30-A. Renders: 'tool: <name>\ninputs: <redacted>\noutcome: <x>'. Full mode requires opt-in because of the higher info density.
close_reason: Deferred to v0.7.x (superseded by ccsc-4nm). Design rationale: MCP's permission-relay protocol has no tool-completion notification — we send permissions/response allow, Claude executes in its own process, no outcome surfaces back through the MCP channel. Shipping a synthesized outcome via proxy heuristics (timer-based 'assumed done', next-action correlation) would produce receipts LESS trustworthy than the authoritative hash-chained journal they project. The pre-exec receipt (Epic 30-B.2 / PR #106) is the complete projection surface; outcome data lives in audit.log and is accessible via --verify-audit-log. Follow-up bead ccsc-4nm will wire the post-exec edit when one of these lands: (a) MCP spec adds a tool-completion notification; (b) we relocate tool execution into server.ts proper to observe outcomes directly; (c) operator-facing need demonstrably requires inventing a lossy status. Memory safety for the now-orphaned auditReceipts map landed in PR #110 (size cap + FIFO eviction).

title: 30-B.4: compact mode — tool name + outcome only (no inputs)
description: Renders: 'tool: <name>  →  <outcome>'. Used when inputs could contain user context that shouldn't be echoed back into the channel.
close_reason: Deferred to v0.7.x (superseded by ccsc-4nm). Design rationale: MCP's permission-relay protocol has no tool-completion notification — we send permissions/response allow, Claude executes in its own process, no outcome surfaces back through the MCP channel. Shipping a synthesized outcome via proxy heuristics (timer-based 'assumed done', next-action correlation) would produce receipts LESS trustworthy than the authoritative hash-chained journal they project. The pre-exec receipt (Epic 30-B.2 / PR #106) is the complete projection surface; outcome data lives in audit.log and is accessible via --verify-audit-log. Follow-up bead ccsc-4nm will wire the post-exec edit when one of these lands: (a) MCP spec adds a tool-completion notification; (b) we relocate tool execution into server.ts proper to observe outcomes directly; (c) operator-facing need demonstrably requires inventing a lossy status. Memory safety for the now-orphaned auditReceipts map landed in PR #110 (size cap + FIFO eviction).

title: 30-B.3: Post-execution hook — edit receipt with outcome
description: After tool execution, chat.update the receipt with exit code, path, summary. Edit (not new message) to keep thread clean. Handle rate-limit on update path.
close_reason: Deferred to v0.7.x (superseded by ccsc-4nm). Design rationale: MCP's permission-relay protocol has no tool-completion notification — we send permissions/response allow, Claude executes in its own process, no outcome surfaces back through the MCP channel. Shipping a synthesized outcome via proxy heuristics (timer-based 'assumed done', next-action correlation) would produce receipts LESS trustworthy than the authoritative hash-chained journal they project. The pre-exec receipt (Epic 30-B.2 / PR #106) is the complete projection surface; outcome data lives in audit.log and is accessible via --verify-audit-log. Follow-up bead ccsc-4nm will wire the post-exec edit when one of these lands: (a) MCP spec adds a tool-completion notification; (b) we relocate tool execution into server.ts proper to observe outcomes directly; (c) operator-facing need demonstrably requires inventing a lossy status. Memory safety for the now-orphaned auditReceipts map landed in PR #110 (size cap + FIFO eviction).

title: 30-B.2: Pre-execution hook — threaded :receipt: post with correlation ID
description: Before tool execution, if audit!=off, post a stub threaded reply with :receipt: emoji + correlation ID in Block Kit context block. Store its ts for edit.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #106 (merged 2026-04-20). Pre-execution audit receipts post when ChannelPolicy.audit is 'compact'/'full', wired at all three pre-exec points (auto_allow + quorum-approved via button + text reply). AuditReceiptContextBlock type, auditReceipts Map, generateCorrelationId, shouldPostAuditReceipt, buildAuditReceiptMessage all landed. 483 tests pass. correlationId on policy.allow journal event closes the correlation loop for dhh.3.

title: 30-B.1: Add audit:'off'|'compact'|'full' field to ChannelPolicy in lib.ts
description: New optional field on per-channel policy. Default 'off' (no projection). 'compact' = tool name + outcome. 'full' = redacted inputs + outcome.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #105 (merged 2026-04-20). AuditMode type + optional audit field on ChannelPolicy landed in lib.ts. Downstream beads (dhh.2/.3 hooks, dhh.4/.5 modes) can now target the stable shape. 471 tests still pass; no consumer yet.

title: 30-A.10: Test — tamper detection via chain verification
description: Write 100 events, flip 1 byte in entry 42's serialized payload, run verifier — must report chain break at 42. Also flip 1 byte in stored hash field — must report break.
close_reason: verifyJournal() + 12 tamper-detection tests + 1000-event E2E chain verification. PR #74 merged. Chain verification per doc §237-261: schema → prevHash continuity → seq monotonicity → recomputed hash match. Returns VerifyResult on first break with lineNumber, seq, ts, reason.

title: 30-A.9: Test — redaction catches all documented token patterns
description: Table test: each known token pattern goes in, [REDACTED:type] comes out. Negative cases: random hex does not get redacted. Include edge cases (tokens inside JSON strings).
close_reason: Table-driven test covering all 6 documented token patterns + 8 negative cases (tightness regressions) + 5 edge cases (JSON-stringified, error-message quotes, all-six-in-one-blob, deeply nested). Coverage-sync assertion ties rows to tokenPatterns() so new patterns force new rows. 19 new tests, 327 total pass. PR #75 merged.

title: 30-A.7: Atomic append (O_APPEND on POSIX) + fsync on rotation boundary
description: Use fs.createWriteStream with {flags:'a'}. fsync once per N events or on rotation. Rotation: size-based (e.g., 100MB → .log.1). Crash-safe.
close_reason: fsync + O_APPEND landed in PR #73. Per-write fh.sync() after every successful fh.write() — crash between buffer and flush can no longer lose tail events. O_APPEND preservation test confirms writer recovers lastHash/nextSeq from pre-existing content and appends at EOF. Design choice: stayed on FileHandle (not createWriteStream) for explicit per-write sync. 2 new tests (296 pass / 0 fail / 626 expect). Gemini: 'well-reasoned, clearly documented, supported by new tests'. No inline findings.

title: 30-A.8: Test — hash chain verifies end-to-end over synthetic 1000-event log
description: Generate 1000 events, write them, read back, recompute hash chain, assert identical to stored hashes. Proves writer's chain is correct.
close_reason: verifyJournal() + 12 tamper-detection tests + 1000-event E2E chain verification. PR #74 merged. Chain verification per doc §237-261: schema → prevHash continuity → seq monotonicity → recomputed hash match. Returns VerifyResult on first break with lineNumber, seq, ts, reason.

title: 30-A.6: --audit-log-file CLI flag + SLACK_AUDIT_LOG env var
description: Opt-in. If unset, no journal writes. If set, validate path writable, open append-only handle at startup. Env var for deployment, flag for local runs.
close_reason: Journal CLI flag + env var wiring landed in PR #72. resolveJournalPath() pure resolver with flag > env > null priority and flag-shape rejection (addressed Gemini 🟡 Medium: --audit-log-file --debug no longer eats --debug as path). Server.ts: journal module-var init in main(), system.boot event on open, system.shutdown event in shutdown() with trigger reason. 11 new tests (294 pass / 0 fail / 620 expect). Gemini: 'well-implemented ... clean ... comprehensive unit tests ... excellent'. Follow-up beads wire specific gate/policy/session call sites to journal.writeEvent().

title: 30-A.5: Input truncation — first 200 + omitted + last 200 (>500 chars)
description: Large inputs get collapsed. Pattern: first 200 chars + ' … (N chars omitted) … ' + last 200. Preserves correlation with tool error messages while bounding log size.
notes: Design-doc vs bead drift: bead says 'first 200 + omitted + last 200', doc §200-210 says 'hard limit 2048 chars with [... truncated N chars] marker + sibling <field>.len'. Following doc per design-is-authoritative rule. The sandwich-style variant can be a follow-up if desired.
close_reason: Truncation landed in PR #71. truncate() deep-walker with <key>.len sibling on objects, inline marker on strings. truncateEventFields() writer helper. TRUNCATION_LIMIT_DEFAULT = 2048. Writer integration: redact→truncate→hash→write (doc §206). 19 new tests (283 pass). Gemini: 'robust and well-tested ... clean implementation'. Addressed 🟡 Medium (isRecord type-guard refactor across 3 sites) and 🟢 Low (toMatch→toBe test tightening). Rejected 🟢 Low on hasReason/hasInput — deliberate typeof-based guard to distinguish undefined from empty-string. Also noted design-doc-vs-bead drift: bead said sandwich-style, doc says hard-cap+marker+sibling; went with doc.

title: 30-A.3: Trusted-anchor timestamp (ISO-8601 UTC + monotonic counter)
description: ts = { wall: ISO-8601 UTC string, seq: monotonic uint64 }. Counter breaks ties inside same millisecond. Prevents adversarial re-ordering within a single clock tick.
notes: Effectively already implemented by 5pi.1 (JournalEvent.ts as ISO-8601 string with ms precision) + 5pi.2 (JournalWriter increments a monotonic seq per write that disambiguates ties within a clock tick). The design-doc ts/seq pair matches the bead's intent. Separately, the TRUSTED_ANCHOR concept from audit-journal-architecture.md §76-85 (anchor-in-first-event-body) is NOT yet enforced — writer generates an anchor as prevHash but doesn't write it into the event body. If that's the missing piece, file a new bead; otherwise this one can close.

title: Property-based tests for gate() via fast-check — pre-ccsc-u41 safety net
description:
**Problem:** Phase 4 of ccsc-53g refactors gate() — the primary inbound security boundary. Existing tests cover known cases (669 unit + 10 Gherkin scenarios + 85% mutation). Property-based testing catches edge cases in *input space* that specific test cases can't.

**Solution:** fast-check generators for Slack event shapes. Assert invariants:
- Any event with bot_id where allowBotIds doesn't include it → action === 'drop'
- Any event without user field → action === 'drop'  
- Any event matching PERMISSION_REPLY_RE from a bot → action === 'drop'
- Any event from banned user → action === 'drop'
- DM from user not in allowFrom with dmPolicy='allowlist' → action === 'drop'

~5-10 property tests, each generating 100+ random inputs.

**Why this blocks ccsc-u41:** gate() refactor extracts handleBotEvent, handleDmEvent, handleChannelEvent. A behavior drift in edge cases could slip past existing tests but be caught by property tests.

**Acceptance:**
- devDep: fast-check
- New file: features/gate-properties.test.ts (or similar)
- 5+ property tests on gate()
- All pass on current gate() (baseline)
- bun test count grows to ~674+
- Stryker score on lib.ts does not drop

**Effort:** 2 hours
**Risk:** Low (additive — pure new tests)
close_reason: Shipped in commit 3b8bb67. 13 fast-check property tests / 2,600 assertions across 11 gate() invariants (self-echo triple-check, allowBotIds opt-in, PERMISSION_REPLY_RE, no-user drop, non-file-share subtype, DM allowlist, channel gate). Test suite grew 669→682, 0 regressions, full CI gate chain green (typecheck, biome, harness-hash, gherkin-lint, depcruise).

title: Wire Husky + lint-staged pre-commit hook for quality gates
description:
**Problem:** Existing .git/hooks only run beads sync. Broken code can be committed; only CI catches it. This is a gap before the ccsc-53g refactor — typos in extractions reach CI before the developer notices.

**Solution:** Husky 9 + lint-staged. Pre-commit runs on staged files only:
- tsc --noEmit (typecheck)
- biome check --staged
- crap-score on touched files (optional)

**Why this first:** Universal benefit — protects every commit from this point forward, including the 5 ccsc-53g refactor commits.

**Acceptance:**
- .husky/pre-commit created
- package.json has husky + lint-staged devDeps
- 'prepare' script auto-installs hooks on bun install
- Committing a broken .ts file is rejected locally
- Works within beads hook marker framework (don't stomp bd hooks)

**Effort:** 30 min
**Risk:** Low

title: Refactor 4 complexity outliers + tighten crap-score threshold to 30
description:
## Context

`scripts/crap-score.ts` (shipped in PR #135 / ccsc-gh0) measures TS-aware cyclomatic complexity across the six production source files. CI currently gates at `--threshold 85` — a regression-only gate tuned to the current ceiling. The Wall 5 ideal per the `/audit-tests` skill is **30**. Four functions today sit above that ideal and block tightening:

| # | File:line | Function | Complexity | Gap to 30 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `lib.ts:1113` | `gate()` | 32 | +2 |
| 2 | `server.ts:2066` | `handleMessage` | 35 | +5 |
| 3 | `server.ts:1777` | `<anon>` — `socket.on('interactive', ...)` handler | 39 | +9 |
| 4 | `server.ts:850` | `<anon>` — `mcp.setRequestHandler(CallToolRequestSchema, ...)` | 84 | +54 |

`lib.ts` outliers: 1 function. `server.ts` outliers: 3 functions (the two anons sit at the Socket Mode → MCP boundary; `handleMessage` is the inbound event dispatcher).

`policy.ts`, `manifest.ts`, `journal.ts`, `supervisor.ts` — all functions are already ≤ 15 complexity. No action needed there.

## Why this matters

Threshold 85 works as a regression gate but lets new functions land at very high complexity without challenge. Tightening to 30 forces every future PR to decompose up-front instead of relying on future refactor passes. It also matches the skill's rubric, so our CRAP gate is directly comparable to other repos using the same audit methodology.

## Per-function decomposition strategy

### 1. `gate()` at `lib.ts:1113` (32 → target ≤ 30)

Structure today (5 inline numbered blocks in the comment):
1. Bot message handling — self-echo + per-channel opt-in + PERMISSION_REPLY_RE
2. Drop non-message subtypes
3. No user_id → drop
4. DM handling — allowlist check, dmPolicy branch, pairing code lookup, pending-cap check, new code generation
5. Channel handling — opt-in + allowFrom + requireMention

**Proposed extractions (all pure, all testable in isolation):**
- `handleBotEvent(ev, opts): GateResult | null` — returns a `drop` result when the event is a self-echo / not-opted-in / permission-reply-shaped, returns `null` when the bot event should fall through to normal channel-policy checks. Removes branch depth ~8 from `gate()`.
- `handleDmEvent(ev, opts): GateResult` — extract the entire `channel_type === 'im'` block including pairing-code issuance. Removes branch depth ~9.

After extraction `gate()` becomes a short top-level router (~10 complexity):

```ts
if (ev.bot_id) { const r = handleBotEvent(ev, opts); if (r) return r }
if (ev.subtype && ev.subtype !== 'file_share') return { action: 'drop' }
if (!ev.user) return { action: 'drop' }
if (ev.channel_type === 'im') return handleDmEvent(ev, opts)
return handleChannelEvent(ev, opts)
```

**Risk: HIGH.** `gate()` is the primary inbound security boundary. Coverage: `server.test.ts` has 30+ tests around `gate()`; `features/steps/gate.ts` runs all 10 scenarios from `inbound_gate.feature` against it. Refactor must keep both suites green bit-for-bit, including Stryker survivors killed in ccsc-y4e. Sub-agent review before merge is strongly advised.

### 2. `handleMessage` at `server.ts:2066` (35 → target ≤ 30)

Today: a single `switch(result.action)` on gate's return, with `drop` / `pair` / `deliver` branches. The `deliver` branch is ~60 lines and does: thread-tracking, journal write, file download, text rewrite, MCP notification. That's the bulk of the complexity.

**Proposed extraction:**
- `deliverEvent(ev, access, ...): Promise<void>` — move the entire `case 'deliver':` body into its own async function. `handleMessage` collapses to a thin dispatcher over the three action kinds.

**Risk: MEDIUM.** Pure refactor; no behavior change. Coverage is integration-shaped through the socket.on('message') callbacks which are harder to exercise in unit tests, but existing tests on gate + libDeliveredThreadKey + mcp.notification flows still hold.

### 3. `socket.on('interactive', ...)` anon at `server.ts:1777` (39 → target ≤ 30)

Today: handles Block Kit button interactions (`perm:allow:<id>` / `perm:deny:<id>` / `perm:more:<id>`). Big branch on `verb` + access.allowFrom gate + pendingPermissions lookup + approval aggregation + message update.

**Proposed extraction:**
- `handlePermissionAction(body, match): Promise<void>` — move the entire body after `await ack()` into a named function. The anon becomes a 5-line wrapper.
- Further split `handlePermissionAction` into `handleMoreAction` / `handleAllowAction` / `handleDenyAction` if the extracted function is still over 30. Profile before splitting further — a single extraction may be enough.

**Risk: MEDIUM-HIGH.** This is the permission-approval surface. NIST two-person integrity tests (policy.ts + server.test.ts end-to-end) must stay green.

### 4. `mcp.setRequestHandler(CallToolRequestSchema, ...)` anon at `server.ts:850` (84 → target ≤ 30)

Today: the master `switch(name)` for every MCP tool. Currently dispatches at least 6 tools (reply, upload, set_chunk_policy, set_chunk_mode, list_sessions, publish_manifest based on prior inspection) — each `case` is itself ~30-50 lines.

**Proposed extraction (larger surgery):**
- Lift each `case 'X': { ... }` body into its own `executeX(args, ctx): Promise<MCPResult>` function. The anon becomes a dispatch table: `const handler = handlers[name]; return handler ? handler(args, ctx) : unknownTool(name)`.
- Each `executeX` individually should land under 30.
- Optionally: move the handlers to their own file (`tools.ts`) to keep `server.ts` focused on Slack + MCP wiring.

**Risk: MEDIUM.** Largest LOC change but most mechanical — each `case` is already self-contained. The Zod input validation at the top of the anon stays in place.

## Acceptance criteria

- `bun scripts/crap-score.ts --threshold 30` exits 0 (no function above 30)
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` step updated to `--threshold 30` (tightened from 85)
- `bun run typecheck` clean
- `bun test --timeout 15000` — all existing tests pass bit-for-bit (669 as of main; refactor may not change the count)
- `features/steps/*.ts` scenarios all pass against the refactored primitives
- `bash scripts/coverage-floor.sh 95` — coverage does not drop below the existing 98.43% / 98.75%
- Stryker run on lib.ts (ccsc-y4e's tests) still reports ≥ 84% mutation score — no lost kills from the gate() refactor
- `bunx depcruise --config .dependency-cruiser.js .` — 0 violations (31-A.4 etc. still enforced)
- `bash scripts/harness-hash.sh --verify` — OK (no .feature files touched)
- CHANGELOG.md entry under Unreleased / Changed

## Out of scope (explicit)

- Adopting Biome (`ccsc-dz8`) — separate bd
- Wiring Stryker in CI (`ccsc-0mn`) — separate bd
- Moving handlers to `tools.ts` — can be bundled IF the complexity win is large enough, but strictly the bd only requires bringing all four under 30. File-splitting is an additional judgment call.

## Suggested sequencing

1. Start with `server.ts:850` (the 84-complexity MCP dispatch) — largest gap, most mechanical, lowest per-branch risk.
2. Then `server.ts:1777` (interactive handler) — medium complexity, single responsibility.
3. Then `handleMessage` — small extraction of the `deliver` branch.
4. Finish with `gate()` — smallest gap but highest security risk; do it last when you have the most surrounding coverage confidence.
5. Tighten CI threshold to 30 as the final commit of the PR.

Each function refactor can be its own commit on one branch; the CI-threshold flip is the closing commit.

## References

- `scripts/crap-score.ts` — the measurement tool (PR #135 / ccsc-gh0)
- `000-docs/QUALITY_GATES.md` §8c — the Wall 5 gate row
- `000-docs/MUTATION_REPORT.md` — Stryker baseline that must be preserved
- `features/inbound_gate.feature` + `features/steps/gate.ts` — Wall 1 scenarios against `gate()`
- `server.test.ts` — unit + integration coverage (669 tests at time of bd)
- `~/.claude/skills/audit-tests/references/crap-and-complexity.md` — the skill's Wall 5 rubric

## Risk posture

**gate() is security-critical.** Any diff that touches it should:
- Ship with sub-agent architecture review (code-reviewer or architect-reviewer)
- Keep every test green bit-for-bit — no "I'll fix that test to match the new behavior" moves
- Re-run Stryker post-refactor and confirm ≥ 84% kept on lib.ts
- Not change the public API shape of `GateResult` or `GateOptions` — those are contracts
design: Strategy per function: (1) gate() at lib.ts:1113 → extract handleBotEvent() + handleDmEvent() as pure helpers; gate() becomes a thin 5-line router. (2) handleMessage at server.ts:2066 → extract deliverEvent() for the case 'deliver' body. (3) socket.on('interactive') anon at server.ts:1777 → extract handlePermissionAction() past the ack() call; further split into per-verb handlers if needed. (4) CallToolRequestSchema anon at server.ts:850 → lift each switch case into executeX() handlers; anon becomes a dispatch-table lookup. Sequencing: tackle largest gap first (server.ts:850 at 84), finish with highest-risk (gate() at 32).
acceptance_criteria: All four over-30 functions (gate, handleMessage, server.ts:1777 anon, server.ts:850 anon) drop below complexity 30 as measured by scripts/crap-score.ts. CI step in .github/workflows/ci.yml flipped from --threshold 85 to --threshold 30. All existing tests green bit-for-bit (bun test: 669 pass, 0 fail). Coverage ≥ 98.37% line / 98.75% func. Stryker re-run on lib.ts still ≥ 84% mutation score (no lost kills from the gate refactor). features/steps/gate.ts scenarios all pass. harness-hash verify OK. depcruise 0 violations. CHANGELOG entry under Unreleased/Changed.
notes:
## Phase Breakdown (2026-04-21)

This epic is broken into 5 sequenced child bds:

| Phase | ID | Function | Complexity | Status |
|-------|-----|----------|------------|--------|
| 1 | ccsc-lry | MCP dispatch (server.ts:850) | 84→9 | READY |
| 2 | ccsc-upx | Interactive handler (server.ts:1786) | 39→15 | READY |
| 3 | ccsc-530 | handleMessage (server.ts:2087) | 35→14 | READY |
| 4 | ccsc-u41 | gate() (lib.ts:1095) | 32→12 | BLOCKED (1-3) |
| 5 | ccsc-510 | CI threshold 85→30 | N/A | BLOCKED (1-4) |

**Parallelization:** Phases 1, 2, 3 can run in parallel (no shared code). Phase 4 waits for all three for max coverage confidence. Phase 5 is the final commit.

**Estimated effort:** 9-12 hours total.

**Plan document:** ~/.claude/plans/ccsc-53g-complexity-refactor.md
close_reason: Epic complete. All 5 phases shipped: ccsc-lry (MCP dispatch 84→5), ccsc-upx (interactive 39→7), ccsc-530 (handleMessage 35→8), ccsc-u41 (gate 32→6), ccsc-510 (CI threshold 85→30). crap-score: 0 functions over threshold 30. Test suite 682 pass, coverage 98.39%/98.75%, all 9 CI gates green.

title: Test-suite audit: post-Epic-31 health check
description: The bot-manifest protocol (issue #31) just landed with 14 PRs and 94 new tests. Before starting the next epic, audit the test suite quality: bias detection, assertion density, CRAP complexity, architecture-rule coverage beyond the 31-A.4 import-graph check, and Quality Gate Sweep (lint/format/types/security/secrets). Produce TEST_AUDIT.md + QUALITY_GATES.md. Do NOT auto-remediate without approval — surface findings for Jeremy's call on what to fix next.
notes: Triggered by /audit-tests after session that shipped 14 PRs (111→124). Context: ~594 tests in a single-file bun:test suite, typecheck + test are required CI, no mutation testing yet, no formal CRAP tooling, import-graph constraint exists ad-hoc in server.test.ts (31-A.4) rather than via dependency-cruiser.
close_reason: Test audit shipped as 000-docs/TEST_AUDIT.md. Headline: suite in excellent health — 594 pass, 98.4% line / 98.8% function coverage, 0 skips/only/todo, 2.47 assertions/test, 104 negative-path .toThrow() calls. No P0 findings. Real gaps are mature-project polish: no linter (TS strict catches most), no mutation testing (would cost CI time), no formal dependency-cruiser (31-A.4 invariant already enforced via import-graph test). Highest-leverage next moves documented: (1) coverage floor in CI, (2) migrate 31-A.4 to dependency-cruiser + keep regex backup, (3) ccsc-80e timeout fix. No auto-remediation performed — audit report only, for Jeremy's call on next priority.

title: Flaky: verifyJournal 1000-event chain test times out under I/O contention
description: The test 'verifyJournal > ok over a clean 1000-event chain (ccsc-5pi.8)' runs in ~3s in isolation but can exceed bun test's 5000ms default when the full 523-test suite runs the async disk writes concurrently. First observed on feat/epic31a-manifest-schema-bz-ccsc-s53-1. Fix options: (a) bump the per-test timeout to 10_000 for this one test via test('...', { timeout: 10_000 }, ...); (b) reduce the chain size to 500; (c) batch the writes synchronously inside the test rather than via 1000 per-event awaits. (a) is the minimal surgical fix and preserves the end-to-end intent.
notes: Observed locally running full suite on a dev machine; CI may not flake since GH runners are less contended. If CI starts flaking intermittently on this test, treat as load-bearing.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #127: per-test timeout bumped to 15_000 on the 'ok over a clean 1000-event chain (end-to-end, ccsc-5pi.8)' test via bun:test's 3rd-arg timeout signature. Verified locally: bun test -t 'ccsc-5pi.8' passes in ~2.8s (well under 15s), full suite 594/594 pass in 3.2s, typecheck clean. Commit 045c24b; merged in dea984d.

title: /slack-channel:policy skill for rule authoring without hand-editing access.json
description: v0.6.0 ships the policy engine but operators must hand-edit access.json.policy to author rules. Add a /slack-channel:policy CLI skill (sibling of /slack-channel:configure and /slack-channel:access) that: (1) lists current rules with effect + match summary, (2) adds a rule (auto_approve/deny/require_approval) with validation via parsePolicyRules(), (3) removes by id, (4) lints for shadowing (detectShadowing) and broad auto_approve (detectBroadAutoApprove) before write, (5) atomic write with mode 0o600, (6) reload notice (hot reload is deferred per v0.6.0 release plan §R3 — for now, operator must restart the server). Scope: skill file + handler; no server-side reload logic. Complements the hand-edit path, does not replace it. Ships v0.6.1.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #144 (merge commit b26f72f) on 2026-04-21. New /slack-channel:policy skill at skills/policy/SKILL.md with four subcommands (list, lint, add <id> <effect> <json-match> [opts], remove <id>). Atomic 0o600 writes; pre-write validation via new scripts/policy-validate.ts wrapper that imports real parsePolicyRules() + detectShadowing() + detectBroadAutoApprove() from policy.ts (same functions server uses at boot). Validator also implements the duplicate-id check ACCESS.md §'Safety checks' documents — server.ts loadPolicy() does not enforce this today, drift tracked in ccsc-kx8. CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased], CLAUDE.md skills list, and ACCESS.md all updated to reference the new surface. No server-side changes; hot reload remains intentionally unsupported. All 9 CI gates green (typecheck, Biome, 682 tests, coverage 98.39/98.75%, depcruise, gherkin-lint, harness-hash, bun audit, crap-score max 28/30). Two rounds of Gemini review addressed: round 1 flagged args[0]! non-null assertions (14921ff removed them with early-return narrowing); round 2 flagged 'as Record<string, unknown>' cast (8adc2f6 replaced with 'policy' in body type guard) and argument-hint/[opts] inconsistency (8adc2f6 made argument-hint explicit). Two 🟢 Gemini suggestions on CHANGELOG/ACCESS were false positives (suggested text already present).

title: Review and triage GitHub code-scanning alerts
description: Periodic sweep of https://github.com/jeremylongshore/claude-code-slack-channel/security/code-scanning. Known open: CodeQL alert #20 (js/file-system-race false positive on server.test.ts realpath+statSync in tmpdir test; already resolved as a PR thread, but the security-tab alert needs UI dismissal with 'used in tests' rationale). Walk the full list, dismiss false positives with rationale, file beads for real ones.
notes:
Triage complete 2026-04-19 (end of v0.7.0 session). All three currently open
alerts are non-actionable; attach these notes to the UI dismissal.

---

### Alert #19 — ERROR · DangerousWorkflowID · .github/workflows/gemini-review.yml:46
**Dismiss reason:** won't fix

> Accepted risk with documented mitigations. The `pull_request_target` + head-SHA
> checkout pattern is intentional so fork PRs receive AI review (see CHANGELOG
> v0.4.0, #34). Mitigations in place: (1) `persist-credentials: false` — no
> write token available to PR code; (2) checkout by SHA not ref — blocks
> force-push TOCTOU; (3) only action executed is `run-gemini-cli`, which is a
> read-only AI reviewer with restricted shell tools; (4) workflow is read-only
> by design. The GitHub SecurityLab post this rule cites addresses the classic
> "pwn request" pattern where PR code gets a write token or can execute
> arbitrary build steps — neither applies here.

### Alert #20 — warning · js/file-system-race · server.test.ts:3157
**Dismiss reason:** used in tests

> Test fixture, not production code. The flagged pattern is `statSync(p)` →
> `readFileSync(p)` inside a unit test that just created the file via
> `saveSession()` moments earlier. No concurrent writer exists in the test
> harness between the stat (mode check) and read (content check), so there is
> no real TOCTOU window. Flagging this would require either inlining a single
> `readFileSync` + mode-from-stat call (harms test clarity) or adding
> file-descriptor plumbing to the test, both of which add complexity for no
> security gain in a test-only context.

### Alert #21 — warning · js/file-system-race · journal.ts:414
**Dismiss reason:** won't fix

> Out of threat model. The flagged line is `fsOpen(opts.path, 'a', 0o600)` in
> JournalWriter initialization. CodeQL fires because the earlier reverse-chunk
> read (to recover lastHash and nextSeq from the existing audit log) is
> followed by this open-for-append, creating a stat-then-use window. A real
> exploit would require an attacker with write access to
> `~/.claude/channels/slack/` (mode 0o700, single-writer by design). An
> attacker inside that directory already has write access to `.env` (tokens),
> `access.json` (allowlist), and `sessions/*` — swapping the audit log with a
> symlink is strictly less powerful than what they already possess. Per
> THREAT-MODEL.md, write access to the state directory is outside the attack
> surface we defend against. Bead ccsc-otd tracks restructuring this open path
> to do the hash-chain read via a single file handle, which would incidentally
> close this CodeQL path.

---

**Action tomorrow:** UI dismiss all three with the reasons/comments above,
then close this bead. API dismissal was attempted and blocked by sandbox
(scope: "comment" ≠ "dismiss") — this is intentional friction, not a bug.
close_reason: Triaged 5 open code-scanning alerts on 2026-04-21 (post-v0.8.0). All dismissed via gh api PATCH with documented rationales (≤280-char cap): #19 DangerousWorkflowID (gemini-review.yml, won't fix — intentional pull_request_target+SHA pattern, no write token); #20 js/file-system-race (server.test.ts:4630, used in tests — no concurrent writer in test harness); #22 js/file-system-race (journal.ts:421, won't fix — out of threat model, state dir mode 0o700 single-writer); #23/#24 js/file-access-to-http + js/http-to-file-access (server.ts:1164-1170, false positive — isSlackFileUrl gate + sanitizeFilename + fixed INBOX_DIR, re-fire of dismissed #15/#16 after line shift). Verified: zero open alerts remain. Earlier claim that API dismissal required UI was wrong — repo scope is sufficient.

title: 30-A.17: Record TRUSTED_ANCHOR in first system.boot event body
description: Doc §76-85 says the random TRUSTED_ANCHOR must be written as the payload of the very first system.boot event so it cannot be forged without discovering it from the journal itself. Today the writer generates a random genesis prevHash but the server bootstrap (server.ts:1114) does not pass one in and does not record it in the boot event's input/reason. Fix: in server.ts, generate the anchor, pass it to JournalWriter.open({genesisAnchor}), and include it in the boot event body (input: { trustedAnchor: '<hex>' }). Surfaces Epic 30-A doc-compliance gap caught during 5pi.3 rollup.
notes: Blocks full Epic 30-A closure. Parent: ccsc-5pi.12.
close_reason: TRUSTED_ANCHOR now generated on fresh chains, pinned as initialPrevHash, AND recorded in system.boot event input.trustedAnchor. 6 new tests (shape, uniqueness, schema acceptance, pin-and-record round-trip, verifyJournal acceptance, anchor-in-body tamper detection). 333/0 tests. PR #77 merged.

title: Gemini review still no-ops — add mcpServers block to workflow
description: After ccsc-304 the prompt is passed correctly (seen in PR #62 gemini log line 572) but Gemini still posts no review. Root cause: the workflow's settings block names GitHub PR tools (pull_request_read, add_comment_to_pending_review, pull_request_review_write) but doesn't declare the GitHub MCP server that provides them. Gemini has nowhere to call them, so the review tools are unreachable. Fix: add an mcpServers block pointing at ghcr.io/github/github-mcp-server with the GITHUB_TOKEN env, matching the canonical google-github-actions/run-gemini-cli review example.
close_reason: Ported working Gemini review pattern from x-bug-triage-plugin + braves. Added mcpServers.github block with ghcr.io/github/github-mcp-server container, moved includeTools under mcpServers, switched prompt to slash-command /gemini-review form, added .gemini/commands/gemini-review.toml with threat-model-grounded review priorities, required env vars (GITHUB_TOKEN, ISSUE_*, PULL_REQUEST_NUMBER, REPOSITORY), workflow_name, maxSessionTurns 25, concurrency group, timeout 7min. Kept pull_request_target + head-sha checkout for fork safety. Merged PR #64. 210 tests still pass. Next PR opened will be first real test.

title: Fix gemini-review workflow — add missing prompt input
description: The gemini-review.yml workflow passes tool settings but no prompt input. The run-gemini-cli action defaults prompt to 'You are a helpful assistant.' so Gemini runs with no task and leaves no comments. Every PR for the last 10+ has had a no-op review. Fix: add a prompt telling Gemini to fetch the PR diff via pull_request_read, post inline comments via add_comment_to_pending_review, and submit with pull_request_review_write.
close_reason: Added prompt input to gemini-review.yml. Merged PR #61. Next PR opened will be first real test of new prompt (pull_request_target reads base-ref workflow).

title: 30-A.15: --verify-audit-log CLI subcommand

Design doc 000-docs/audit-journal-architecture.md references a verify command but no bead filed it. Surface as a CLI flag: server.ts --verify-audit-log <path> reads the journal, re-hashes the chain, reports first tamper position or OK.

Depends on the hash-chain writer (30-A earlier beads) landing first.

Acceptance:
- CLI: bun server.ts --verify-audit-log ~/.claude/channels/slack/audit.log
- Exit 0 on intact chain, non-zero with first-tampered-line number on mismatch
- Works on partial journals (truncated tail is not tamper)
- Documented in audit-journal-architecture.md
